title: Dry oil body serum
subtitle: A fast-absorbing dry oil blend with rosehip and jojoba
excerpt: A dry oil body serum: rosehip oil, jojoba oil, and sweet almond oil blended with rose geranium essential oil. Anhydrous and preservative-free. Dry oils absorb quickly without a heavy residue; rosehip provides linoleic acid for skin barrier support; jojoba stabilises the blend's shelf life.
sourceNotes: USDA AOCS fatty-acid database, rosehip and jojoba fatty-acid profiles. Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014), p. 389, rose geranium dermal leave-on limits.

recipe.batchNotes: Doubles to 200 ml; the only consideration is the amber glass bottle size.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready immediately after blending. Shelf life 6-9 months.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 6-9 months in an amber glass bottle in a cool, dark cupboard. Rosehip oil has a relatively high iodine value and can go rancid within 6 months if stored warm or in sunlight. Amber glass protects from UV. Discard if the oil develops a rancid, sharp, or paint-like smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: Contains no water. Anhydrous oil blends keep without a preservative.

content.text: A dry oil body serum. Rosehip oil at 30% provides linoleic acid and beta-carotene for skin barrier support; jojoba oil at 40% is technically a liquid wax rather than a true oil, which gives it exceptional stability and a dry, non-greasy skin feel; sweet almond oil at 30% bridges the two and provides oleic acid for emolliency. Rose geranium essential oil at 2% provides the fragrance and rounds the blend.
and requires no preservative. The jojoba component stabilises the rosehip oil's shelf life; a 100% rosehip serum would go rancid in 3-4 months, but the jojoba-dominant blend holds 6-9 months.

items.prepNote: golden or clear jojoba oil. 40% of the blend. Longest-lasting of the carrier oils; stabilises the rosehip.
cold-pressed rosehip oil. 30% of the blend. Rich in linoleic acid (an essential fatty acid). Refrigerated rosehip oil keeps longer; store the finished serum in a cool location.
sweet almond oil. 28% of the blend. Light and emollient.
Pelargonium graveolens rose geranium essential oil. 2% of the batch (approximately 2 g ≈ 2 ml). Within the IFRA leave-on limit for body products.

content.text: Weigh all oils and the essential oil into a clean, dry bowl or jug. Stir gently to combine; no heat is needed, all three carrier oils are liquid at room temperature. Pour into a clean, dry amber glass bottle and fit the pump dispenser. Label with the batch date.
That is the entire active work for this recipe. No heating, no emulsifying, no cooling phase. The complexity is in sourcing quality oils and knowing the ratios; the making itself takes 15 minutes.

Apply 4-6 pumps to damp skin immediately after a shower while the skin still holds moisture. The damp-skin application significantly improves oil absorption and avoids the heavy feel of oil on completely dry skin. Massage in with long strokes and allow to absorb for 1-2 minutes before dressing.

Apply a small amount to the inside of the wrist and wait 24-48 hours before wider use. Rosehip oil can occasionally trigger sensitivity in people with rose or rose-family pollen allergies; if redness or itching occurs, substitute rosehip oil with argan oil at the same weight.

6-9 months in an amber glass bottle in a cool, dark cupboard or the fridge. Rosehip oil is the limiting ingredient; it has a high linoleic acid content which makes it more prone to oxidative rancidity than oleic-acid-dominant oils. Amber glass protects from UV; cool temperature slows oxidation. Discard if the serum develops a sharp, rancid, or paint-like smell.
